interactions.summary: Tucker introduces Jiang as someone with a record of predictions and asks where the Iran war is going, how it will resolve, and what consequences it will have.
Jiang thanks Tucker and Tucker acknowledges him before the substantive answer begins.

Jiang predicts an Iran war resembling Ukraine: attritional, difficult to stop, energy-focused, globally disruptive, and likely to draw in America, the Strait of Hormuz, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, and other actors.
Tucker accepts the scenario as near-worst-case and asks why major powers that would be hurt by it cannot settle the war quickly.
Jiang says the war develops its own momentum because a ceasefire would require American retreat, Iranian regional security guarantees, GCC dependence on Iran, and pressure on the petrodollar system.

claims.claim: Jiang predicts the Iran war will become a years-long war of attrition, with neither side conceding even when a ceasefire would be materially rational.
claims.temporal_scope: Prediction made in the 2026-03-20 Tucker Carlson interview.

claims.claim: Jiang ties the war's global impact to cheap energy: attacks on Iranian and GCC energy infrastructure, possible $200 oil, fuel rationing, and food shortage risks would stress the world economy.
Jiang says the loss of an Iranian pragmatic negotiator removes an off-ramp and locks both sides into attritional commitment.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's dated reading of the war leadership situation on 2026-03-20.

claims.claim: Jiang argues the United States lacks a viable ceasefire path because Iranian terms would imply American withdrawal from the Middle East, GCC realignment toward Iran, and damage to the petrodollar recycling system.
claims.temporal_scope: Geopolitical model stated in the 2026-03-20 interview.

signature_moments.moment: War acquires its own momentum and logic, so actors become trapped by the conditions that would be required to stop it.
signature_moments.source_phrase: it achieves a momentum and a logic of its own
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This phrase becomes Jiang's governing model for why rational incentives do not automatically produce peace.

signature_moments.moment: The assassination of a pragmatic off-ramp turns a war that should end into a long attritional commitment.
signature_moments.source_phrase: There really is no more off-ramp
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the read a concrete hinge between event chronology and structural war logic.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A politically viable exit from war; Jiang argues the Iran war loses such exits once ceasefire terms threaten American regional order.
The GCC practice of selling oil in U.S. dollars and recycling proceeds into the American economy.

chronology_notes.note: This is Jiang's 2026-03-20 forecast of an Iran-centered attritional war and its petrodollar consequences.

uncertainty_notes.note: The transcript renders several names and phrases roughly; preserve the claim structure without over-normalizing the proper nouns.
